title: With broken heart and contrite sigh
firstLine: With broken heart and contrite sigh,
publicDomainBasis: Cornelius Elven (1797–1873), William Batchelder Bradbury (1816–1868). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
With broken heart and contrite sigh,
A trembling sinner, Lord, I cry;
Thy pard’ning grace is rich and free:
O God, be merciful to me!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
I smite upon my troubled breast,
With deep and conscious guilt oppressed;
Christ and His cross my only plea:
O God, be merciful to me!

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Far off I stand with tearful eyes,
Nor dare uplift them to the skies;
But Thou dost all my anguish see,
O God, be merciful to me!

sections.label: Verse 4
Verse 5
sections.text:
And when redeemed from sin and hell,
With all the ransomed throng I dwell,
My raptured song shall ever be,
God has been merciful to me!
